mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months where PAYEMS increases by more than 50k workers over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and credit costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate reached 2.19% in Oct 2018
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) interact to tighten real financial conditions and erode corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: rising inflation with tight monetary policy
failure_condition: The interaction term between month-over-month changes in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS level exhibits a Rank IC below 0.03 or a t-statistic below 2.0 for predicting 5-day forward returns over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions when unemployment is already low, a rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals deteriorating labor market momentum that precedes earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 3.5% and UNRATE increases > 0.2pp over prior 3 months
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.2pp while below 4.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of such episodes over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening residential investment and forward-looking consumer durables demand, which drags on cyclical earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines are followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of quarterly observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates rising capacity utilization and manufacturing profitability, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) interact to squeeze corporate margins through high wage costs and weak top-line growth, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The RSAFS-UNRATE interaction term fails to achieve a t-statistic greater than 2.0 or Rank IC above 0.03 for predicting 20-day returns over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month retail sales growth (RSAFS) exceeding 0.5% signals a positive demand surprise that lifts cyclical sectors and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS grows more than 0.5% month-over-month, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances.
